reason: Live-verified contract (codex-cli 0.142.5, same as container pin): hooks.json schema, decision:block isomorphism, stop_hook_active, --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust mandatory (silent skip without it), fail-open crash behavior, env inheritance, session_id stable across resume. Every design decision traces here.

Approved design (user 2026-07-09): §2a helpers move to agent/loadout.py, §2b codex materialize+argv, §2c snippet timeout 30, §2d COMPANY_WAKE_ID nonce, §4 tradeoffs (trust flag ALWAYS on — silent skip is the failure mode to kill).
Ordered execution plan with per-step validation commands and rollback points A/B. Follow steps 1-6; step 7 (container e2e) is run by the main session afterwards.

Source of the hooks helpers to move (§2a: _snippet_hooks/_merge_hooks/_remove_hooks → loadout.py public names); its materialize_home hooks-reconcile flow is the template codex must mirror symmetrically. Behavior must stay byte-identical (golden test).

Target adapter: replace materialize_home step-4 TODO with the symmetric reconcile into CODEX_HOME/hooks.json; build_argv gains --dangerously-bypass-hook-trust unconditionally; parse_output gets only a comment (2 non-fatal error items already covered).

Neutral core receiving the moved helpers (they operate on any JSON file with a top-level hooks key: claude settings.json / codex hooks.json); manifest hooks slot (write_manifest/read_manifest) already exists.
§2c: add "timeout": 30 to the hook entry (codex default 600s would eat the wake budget; claude honors timeout too — single snippet feeds both runtimes).

§2d: session_marker_path priority becomes COMPANY_RECORD_MARKER > COMPANY_WAKE_ID (new, env) > current behavior (session_id param / COMPANY_SESSION_ID) > default. record_stop_hook.py itself stays UNCHANGED.

§2d: wake() mints uuid4 nonce, passes env={**os.environ, COMPANY_WAKE_ID: nonce} to subprocess.run on all paths. NB: this file was just touched by 07-09-telemetry-usage (usage in _record_wake) — build on current state.
Regression lock: must pass with ZERO edits after the helpers move (design AC on §2a).
Where step-2/3 unit tests go (hooks render/reconcile/idempotence/degrade + argv flag assertions — existing argv tests will fail-first, update them).
